clinical_coherence.notes: 64F African American presenting with features consistent with high-grade glioma. However, patient is listed as female (sex: female) but has 'Benign prostatic hyperplasia' in PMH and takes tamsulosin for BPH. BPH is biologically impossible in a female patient. This is the same type of sex-discordant error seen in PD-S02.

tool_output_consistency.notes: MRI shows ring-enhancing mass with central necrosis and surrounding edema - classic GBM. MR perfusion shows elevated rCBV (4.8) in enhancing rim. MR spectroscopy confirms high-grade neoplasm pattern (Cho/NAA 9.5). CT body negative. Biopsy confirms IDH-wildtype GBM. Hyponatremia (Na 130) and hypochloremia (Cl 92) noted.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD C71.9 correct. Molecular markers appropriate. Differential and management well-structured.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Classic GBM with ring enhancement, high perfusion, and straightforward pathology. Appropriately calibrated.

demographic_plausibility.notes: Female patient with BPH and tamsulosin is biologically impossible. Otherwise, age 64 is appropriate for GBM.

educational_value.notes: Good GBM case with comprehensive workup. Hyponatremia could be a teaching point for SIADH or dexamethasone use in brain tumors.

issues_found: Female patient with BPH and tamsulosin - biologically impossible sex-discordant error
Lab interpretation mentions HbA1c 7.2% but actual value appears to be 6.6%
